findings.title: Critical Dashboard Load Time Regression
findings.description: The dashboard load time has regressed significantly from 1.2s to 4.8s after the v3.2 deployment, severely impacting user experience, especially during incidents.
assumptions: Users rely on the dashboard for real-time monitoring during incidents.

evidence.excerpt: Dashboard load time regressed from 1.2s to 4.8s after v3.2 deploy.
When your dashboard takes 5 seconds to load, I can't use it during an incident.
Monitoring confirms p95 dashboard load time at 4.2s (baseline: 1.2s).

recommendations: Prioritize performance fixes for the dashboard load time.

findings.title: API Latency Degradation
findings.description: API response times have degraded significantly, impacting automated alerting systems and overall user experience.
assumptions: Users depend on timely API responses for alerting and monitoring.

evidence.excerpt: API response times degraded 3x after database migration.
API latency alerts fire — p95 at 350ms (baseline: 120ms).

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the query planner regression in PostgreSQL.

findings.title: WebSocket Memory Leak Issue
findings.description: A memory leak in WebSocket connections leads to server crashes, affecting real-time monitoring capabilities for users.
assumptions: Real-time monitoring is critical for users during incidents.

evidence.excerpt: Memory leak in real-time WebSocket connections.
WebSocket disconnections cause my real-time conversion funnel to go blank.

recommendations: Fix the memory leak in the WebSocket server.

findings.title: Need for Performance Regression Detection
findings.description: There is a strong need for automated performance regression detection in the CI pipeline to prevent future issues.
assumptions: Automated testing can catch performance regressions before deployment.

evidence.excerpt: Implement performance regression detection in CI pipeline.

recommendations: Develop and implement a performance benchmarking suite for CI.

findings.title: Lack of Customer Visibility During Incidents
findings.description: Customers lack visibility into performance metrics during incidents, leading to frustration and potential loss of trust.
assumptions: Customers expect transparency regarding service performance.

evidence.excerpt: Build a public status page that shows real-time p50/p95/p99 latencies.
We almost moved to a competitor.

recommendations: Create a public status page for real-time performance metrics.

summary: The analysis highlights critical performance regressions affecting dashboard load times and API latency, leading to significant user dissatisfaction and potential churn. There is a clear need for automated performance detection and improved customer transparency during incidents to restore trust and enhance user experience.
